mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in GDP signals strong economic demand, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns are not significantly higher when GDPC1 is positive (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Low unemployment indicates a tight labor market, boosting corporate profits and stock prices, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns are not significantly higher when UNRATE is below 4% (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A combination of rising payroll employment and declining unemployment signals a strong labor market, driving corporate earnings growth and positive S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: The joint effect of PAYEMS and UNRATE on SPX returns is not statistically significant (Rank IC < 0.03).

mechanism_narrative: Monetary tightening via rising Fed Funds rates reduces corporate borrowing and consumer spending, leading to negative S&P 500 returns during tightening cycles.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are not significantly lower during periods of FEDFUNDS increases > 0.5% (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Strong industrial production signals robust supply-side activity, supporting corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns are not significantly higher when INDPRO is rising (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: High inflation (CPIAUCSL > 3%) signals central bank intervention, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over 60 days.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns are not significantly lower when CPIAUCSL exceeds 3% (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in retail sales and housing starts signal strong consumer demand, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: The joint effect of RSAFS and HOUST on SPX returns is not statistically significant (Rank IC < 0.03).

mechanism_narrative: A significant decline in industrial production signals weak supply-side conditions, leading to negative S&P 500 returns during supply contractions.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns are not significantly lower when INDPRO declines by >1% (t-test p > 0.05).
